resources.string: Press a moving target
Learn how to create an event to click on a randomly moving target
Click on the moving blue button as fast as possible

Welcome to your second tutorial!

This time, the target is randomly moving on the screen.

First, start the game and check if you can win by yourself.

Difficult, isn't it?
Let's use Klick'r to beat it!

 Click on the Scenario configuration icon to start editing your new scenario.

A Scenario is the root structure of Klick'r automation.

 It contains the general detection settings and a list of Events describing what will be executed when running your Scenario.

Click on the create event button to create your first Event.

An Event describes what Actions (click, swipe…) to execute when certain Conditions are met.

To beat this game, we need to detect the blue character as it moves across the screen. 

Click on the Conditions field to display the Conditions list.

An Image Condition is a part of a screenshot that will be compared to each screen frame of your device.

To beat the game, we want to click on the blue character when it's visible, so we need a Condition that is fulfilled when the character is shown on your device screen.

Click on the create Condition button to create your first Condition.
In this tutorial, we want to learn how to detect an image on the screen, so select Image Condition

The first step of a Screen Condition creation is to take a screenshot for it.\n\n As we want to click when the blue character is visible, the screenshot must contain it. Start the game again in order to display it, and then take the screenshot.
Use the capture button in the floating menu to take the screenshot.
Once the screenshot is taken, you can crop it in order to only get the part that is interesting for the detection.
If your screenshot does not contains the blue character, you can press this button to take a new one.
For this game, the interesting part is the blue character, so crop your screenshot to only get him.\n\nYou can pinch on the screenshot to zoom on it and get a better accuracy for the crop selector.
Ensure the character is complete in the selector. Also try to crop it as close as possible to him in order to ease the image detection.

Your Condition image is now captured!\n\nBy default, the detection type is set to \"Exact\", meaning Klick\'r will only look for it at the capture position. Since the blue character can appear anywhere on the game area, select \"In Area\".
The third option is \"Whole screen\". The bigger than detection is, the slower the detection will be. Keep that in mind when your Scenario execution timing is important.
Click on the area selector to change the detection area.
Resize the detection rectangle to the size of the game area, as the targets won\'t appear outside of it. Once the size is correct, press the validate button to save it.

That's it!
We only needed to define the detection area in order for your Condition to suit our needs.

Click on the save button to register it and return to the Conditions list.
Close the Conditions list to return to the Event configuration.

Your Condition is now configured! With it, the scenario knows when the blue character is visible anywhere on the screen.

However, since the character can appear anywhere, we cannot use a fixed click position — we need to click wherever the Condition is detected.

 Click on the Actions field to display the Actions list.

An Action is something that is executed once the Conditions of its Event are fulfilled.

For this game, we need a Click Action that clicks wherever the blue character is detected. 

Click on the create Action button to create a new Action.
First, we need to pick a type for your Action.\n\nFor this game, we need to click on the blue character, so choose Click to create a new Click Action.

A Click Action is defined by a position and a press duration.

Since the blue character is moving, we cannot use a fixed position. Change the "Click on" setting to "Condition" to click wherever a detected Condition is found.

Select it to use it in your Action.
Click on the Condition selector to choose which detected Condition to click on.\n\nWe need to specify it because an Event can have multiple Conditions.
For our use case it is easy, we only have one Condition, the blue character.\n\nSelect it to click on this Condition when it is detected on your screen.

That's it!
Your Click Action is now correctly configured to beat the game.

Click on the save button to register it and return to the Actions list.
Close the Actions list to return to the Event configuration.
Your Event now has a Condition and an Action, which means it knows what to execute and when to execute it.\n\nClick on the save button to register it and return to the Scenario dialog.

The only requirement for a Scenario is to have at least one Event.
 As our Event already covers how to win the game, we don't need anything else.

Click on the save button to save the Scenario in database and close the configuration dialogs.
As you can see, the play button is now available, meaning that your Scenario is valid and can be used for detection.\n\nClick on it to start the detection, and then start the game.
Congratulations!\n\nYou now know how to track a moving target using the \"Whole screen\" detection type and the \"Click on Condition\" feature!
